epigraph.text:
Plans fail for lack of counsel, but with many 
advisers they succeed.

content.text:
Percent problems typically involve three quantities: the percent, 
the part, and the whole. When we know two of these, we can find 
the third using a percent equation.
The Three Parts of a Percent Problem

items: The **percent** (rate): the ratio expressed as a percent
The **part** (is/amount): a portion of the whole

content.text: When you know the percent and the whole, multiply to find the part.

content.title: Finding the part
content.solution:
Using the percent equation:
\[
 \text{Part} = 35\% \times 80 = 0.35 \times 80 = 28
\]
Therefore, 35\% of 80 is 28.

content.title: Finding the part in context
content.problem:
A survey found that 72\% of 250 students prefer pizza. How many 
students prefer pizza?

content.text: When you know the part and the whole, divide to find the percent.

**Key phrase:** "$p$ is what percent of $n$?" or "What 
percent of $n$ is $p$?"

content.title: Finding percent in context
content.problem:
A student answered 42 questions correctly out of 50. What percent 
did the student answer correctly?

content.text: When you know the part and the percent, divide to find the whole.

content.title: Finding the whole in context
content.problem:
A store sold 36 shirts during a sale, which was 15\% of their 
inventory. How many shirts were in inventory before the sale?
content.solution:
$$
 \text{Whole} &= \frac{36}{15\%} = \frac{36}{0.15} = 240
$$
The store had 240 shirts in inventory.

**Check:** $15\% \times 240 = 0.15 \times 240 = 36$

content.text:
An alternative approach uses the proportion:
\[
 \frac{\text{Part}}{\text{Whole}} = \frac{\text{Percent}}{100}
\]
This method is especially useful when the division seems difficult.

content.title: Using proportions
content.solution:
Set up the proportion:
\[
 \frac{30}{x} = \frac{75}{100}
\]

Cross multiply:
\[
 75x = 30 \times 100 = 3000
\]

Divide both sides by 75:
\[
 x = \frac{3000}{75} = 40
\]

Therefore, 30 is 75\% of 40.

content.title: Identifying types
content.problem:
Identify what is being asked in each problem.

1. What is 45\% of 200? (Finding the part)

2. 18 is what percent of 90? (Finding the percent)

3. 27 is 30\% of what number? (Finding the whole)

content.title: Proportional Giving
content.content:
Scripture describes various forms of giving: the tithe (10\%), 
generous offerings, and sacrificial gifts. Understanding percent 
calculations helps us plan our giving intentionally. If someone 
wants to increase their giving from 10\% to 15\%, they can 
calculate exactly how much additional amount that represents.

marginNotes.text:
The plans of the diligent lead to 
profit as surely as haste leads to poverty.

Suppose one of you has a hundred sheep 
and loses one of them. Doesn't he leave the ninety-nine in the 
open country and go after the lost sheep until he finds it?

marginNotes.author: Isaac Newton
marginNotes.text:
If I have seen further, it is by standing 
on the shoulders of giants.

marginNotes.author: George Pólya
marginNotes.text:
If you can't solve a problem, then there 
is an easier problem you can solve: find it.
